task_summary: Add OAuth2 login with Google and GitHub providers to the Next.js application, including callback handling, session management, and profile linking.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/auth/signin/google redirects to Google's OAuth2 authorization endpoint with correct client_id and scope parameters
GET /api/auth/signin/github redirects to GitHub's OAuth2 authorization endpoint with correct client_id and scope parameters
GET /api/auth/callback/google exchanges the authorization code for tokens and creates a session, then redirects to /dashboard
GET /api/auth/callback/github exchanges the authorization code for tokens and creates a session, then redirects to /dashboard
Invalid or expired authorization codes return a 400 error with a JSON error message, not an unhandled exception
Authenticated users have a valid session cookie that persists across page reloads until explicit logout
GET /api/auth/signout clears the session cookie and redirects to the home page
Users who sign in with both Google and GitHub using the same email address see a single unified account, not duplicate accounts
The /dashboard page returns 401 for unauthenticated requests and 200 with user profile data for authenticated requests
technical_constraints: Next.js 14 App Router — use Route Handlers in app/api/, not Pages Router API routes
PostgreSQL database with Prisma ORM — user and session schemas must be Prisma models
Existing session middleware in src/lib/session.ts must be extended, not replaced
Environment variables for OAuth credentials must be in .env.local (GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID, GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET, GITHUB_CLIENT_ID, GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET)
All auth endpoints must be CSRF-protected using the existing csrf-token pattern in src/lib/csrf.ts
out_of_scope: OAuth providers other than Google and GitHub
Admin panel for managing OAuth client credentials
Rate limiting on auth endpoints (handled by existing middleware)

domain_glossary.authorization code: One-time code returned by OAuth provider after user consent, exchanged for access/refresh tokens server-side
domain_glossary.state parameter: Random string sent with OAuth request and verified on callback to prevent CSRF attacks
domain_glossary.profile linking: Merging OAuth accounts that share the same email into a single user record
domain_glossary.session cookie: HTTP-only cookie containing an encrypted session ID, managed by src/lib/session.ts
